commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=893484517cef5f3869589aa8cfbefaddf868d18c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes session tool descriptions, sessions_send target precedence, sessions_list search normalization, and message.send warning/tests for session-key-looking sends.

PR surface: Source +49, Tests +125. Total +174 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: a message.send result that returns toolResult only receives warning in details, while current source documents details as runtime/UI metadata and model-visible text as content.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof plus a code correction, so it should stay in human review rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(agents): guard session-control routing
target.body:
## Summary

Improves session-control routing guardrails to reduce misrouting between `sessions_send` and `message.send`:

- `sessions_send`: `sessionKey` takes priority when set; redundant `label`/`agentId` fields no longer cause a hard error
- `sessions_list` search: strips leading `#` from search terms for better channel session discoverability
- Tool descriptions updated to explicitly steer inter-session communication toward `sessions_send`
- `message` tool: soft warning when outbound text contains session-key patterns

## Verification

```
pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts           # 38 passed
pnpm test src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts       # 101 passed
pnpm test src/gateway/session-utils.search.test.ts    # 80 passed (4 shards)
pnpm test src/agents/tool-catalog.test.ts             # 3 passed
pnpm test src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts            # 83 passed
pnpm test src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts  # 30 passed
```

## Real behavior proof

After-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw workspace (Node 22, branch `fix/cross-session-control-guardrails`):

**1. `sessions_send` sessionKey priority — redundant label no longer errors:**

```
$ pnpm test src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts -t "ignores label/agentId when sessionKey"
 ✓ sessions_send gating > ignores label/agentId when sessionKey is provided  24ms
 Tests  1 passed | 37 skipped (38)
```

Before this fix, calling `sessions_send` with both `sessionKey` and `label` returned a hard error (`"Provide either sessionKey or label (not both)."`). Now `sessionKey` takes priority and the call succeeds.

**2. `sessions_list` search normalizes leading `#` for channel discovery:**

```
$ pnpm test src/gateway/session-utils.search.test.ts -t "strips leading"
 ✓ gateway-core   > strips leading # from search to match channel session keys  164ms
 ✓ gateway-server > strips leading # from search to match channel session keys  160ms
 ✓ gateway-client > strips leading # from search to match channel session keys  158ms
 ✓ gateway-methods > strips leading # from search to match channel session keys  155ms
```

Searching `#heartbeat` now matches session keys containing `heartbeat` without the literal `#`.

**3. `message.send` session-control warning behavior:**

```
$ pnpm test src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts -t "session-control warning"
 ✓ message tool session-control warning > includes warning when message text contains a session key pattern  8ms
 ✓ message tool session-control warning > does not include warning for normal messages without session keys  1ms
 ✓ message tool session-control warning > does not include warning for non-send actions  0ms
 Tests  3 passed | 98 skipped (101)
```

**4. Runtime tool description now steers models away from using `message.send` for session control:**

```
$ node --import tsx -e "import { createMessageTool } from './src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts'; \
  const tool = createMessageTool({ config: { channels: {} } }); \
  console.log(tool.description.split('\n')[0]);"

Send/delete/manage channel messages. Do NOT use this to communicate with or control another OpenClaw session — use sessions_send instead. Supports actions: broadcast, send.
```

The description actively redirects models to `sessions_send` for cross-session communication.
target.author: melotic
